theme: Xin nghỉ phép & thông báo vắng mặt

vocab.example: Please give at least two days notice for leave requests.
vocab.example_vi: Vui lòng thông báo trước ít nhất hai ngày khi xin nghỉ.

vocab.example: The office is closed for the public holiday next Monday.
vocab.example_vi: Văn phòng đóng cửa vào ngày lễ thứ Hai tới.

vocab.example: I need to take a half day off for a doctor's appointment.
vocab.example_vi: Tôi cần xin nghỉ nửa ngày để đi khám bác sĩ.

vocab.example: This is an emergency — I need to leave right now.
vocab.example_vi: Đây là trường hợp khẩn cấp — tôi cần đi ngay bây giờ.

vocab.example: Can I do remote work instead of taking a full day off?
vocab.example_vi: Tôi có thể làm việc từ xa thay vì nghỉ hẳn một ngày không?

vocab.example: I already added my leave to the team calendar.
vocab.example_vi: Tôi đã thêm ngày nghỉ của mình vào lịch chung của đội.

vocab.example: I will be available on chat even during my leave.
vocab.example_vi: Tôi sẽ có mặt trên chat ngay cả trong lúc nghỉ.

vocab.example: I will return to work on Thursday morning.
vocab.example_vi: Tôi sẽ quay lại làm việc vào sáng thứ Năm.

lines.speaker: Tuan
lines.text: Hoa, sorry to message you suddenly. This is an emergency.

lines.speaker: Tuan
lines.text: My father is in the hospital. I need to take a half day off now.

lines.speaker: Hoa
lines.text: Of course, please go. I know this is short notice, but family comes first.

lines.speaker: Tuan
lines.text: Thank you for understanding. I already added it to the team calendar.

lines.speaker: Hoa
lines.text: Good. Don't worry about work — I'll cover your tasks today.

lines.speaker: Tuan
lines.text: I'll try to be available on chat if anything is really urgent.

lines.speaker: Hoa
lines.text: No need. Just focus on your family. When do you think you'll return?

lines.speaker: Tuan
lines.text: Hopefully tomorrow morning, once things are stable.

questions.q: Why does Tuan need to leave suddenly?
How much time does Tuan ask for at first?
What does Hoa offer to do?

shadowing: Sorry to message you so suddenly, but this is an emergency.
My father is in the hospital, so I need to take a half day off now.
I already added the leave request to the team calendar.
I'll try to be available on chat if anything is really urgent.
Hopefully I'll return to work tomorrow morning, once things are stable.

interview.question: How do you handle an unexpected emergency that affects your work?
tips_vi: Nhấn mạnh việc thông báo nhanh cho quản lý/đồng nghiệp
Thể hiện tinh thần trách nhiệm: bàn giao công việc dù gấp
Có thể kể ví dụ ngắn gọn, chân thực
interview.sample_answer: When an emergency happens, I try to inform my manager or team as quickly as possible, even with very short notice, because I know silence causes more stress for everyone. I explain the situation honestly, without going into too much personal detail, and ask a colleague to cover my most urgent tasks if needed. I also update the team calendar right away, so everyone knows I'm away and can plan around it. For example, when my father was in the hospital, I sent a quick message to my manager and still checked chat briefly in case something critical came up. Once the emergency is under control, I let my team know when I expect to return, so they are not left guessing. I believe clear and quick communication helps reduce the impact on the team, even in a stressful and unexpected situation.

interview.question: How do you balance taking time off with your responsibilities at work?
tips_vi: Nhấn mạnh việc lên kế hoạch trước, không để dồn công việc
Nêu cách bàn giao rõ ràng khi vắng mặt
Thể hiện bạn tôn trọng cả nghỉ ngơi lẫn trách nhiệm công việc
interview.sample_answer: I usually plan my time off well in advance, so I can finish urgent tasks before I leave or ask a colleague to cover them properly. I always update the team calendar and set an out of office message, so people know I'm away and who to contact instead. Before a longer leave, like a public holiday, I also write a short handover note with the status of my current tasks. For real emergencies, when there is no time to plan, I try to stay briefly available on chat, just in case something critical happens, but I keep it to a minimum. I think it's important to rest properly and fully disconnect sometimes, but also to make sure my absence doesn't create problems or extra pressure for the rest of the team.
